$comment: Benchmark tasks for measuring Code Explorer (gitnexus MCP) lift. Each task is a code-navigation/impact question an agent must answer about THIS codebase (code-buddy/src, which gitnexus has indexed). ground_truth is filled in after manual verification (grep + reading) so correctness can be judged independently of the graph itself. Keep tasks graph-favourable but realistic — things a developer actually asks.

tasks.prompt: In this codebase, list every symbol affected if I change the function executePlan. Give the full transitive set (downstream callees and upstream callers), not just direct mentions.

ground_truth.notes: Confirm against `gitnexus impact executePlan` AND a manual caller trace before trusting.

tasks.prompt: Who calls getModelToolConfig in this codebase, and what does that function itself call? List callers and callees.

ground_truth.notes: prompt-builder.ts calls it at least twice.

tasks.prompt: Are there any circular dependencies among the modules in src/context/? If so, list each cycle precisely.
Where is the workspace/bootstrap context injection implemented — the code that loads AGENTS.md / CODEBUDDY.md into the system prompt at session start? Name the file(s) and the function.
What is the blast radius of changing ConfirmationService in this codebase? Which call sites and modules are affected?

ground_truth.notes: Singleton, widely used — good stress test of transitive impact.

tasks.prompt: Trace the call chain for BootstrapLoader.load: what does it call internally, and who calls it?
